item.title: Carney lands in Australia looking to draw closer to a Commonwealth cousin also grappling with Trump
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney has landed in Australia as he looks to bolster ties with a Commonwealth cousin that is increasingly viewed in Ottawa as a key ally on global challenges — a country that has some newfound importance for Canada amid a lot of uncertainty elsewhere.

item.title: Nervous nations calling Canada's energy minister after Iran strikes
item.description: Energy Minister Tim Hodgson says he's starting to get calls from countries about how Canadian energy producers can fill the gap amid the conflict in Iran, which is driving up oil and gas prices.​

item.title: Iran’s former leader was ‘force for evil,’ defence minister says as he defends Ottawa’s backing of air strikes
item.description: Responding to Liberal critics of government’s stance on U.S.-Israel attacks, David McGuinty says party is a big tent with broad range of views

item.title: Toronto’s mayor wants to finally fix this foul-smelling city eyesore. It’s unclear how much it will cost
item.description: Mayor Olivia Chow is recommending Toronto bring control of its sidewalk trash cans in-house after 20 years of privatization.

item.title: Toronto reveals steep cost to deal with failing water meters
‘Transformational.’ Wealthy donor announces $25 million for a lab to fight Toronto homelessness
Doug Ford’s new Science Centre: How the architects came up with the design of the $1B building
No time for truth in this reset with India
item.description: Mark Carney keeps away from reporters in India to avoid questions about foreign interference

item.title: Poilievre lands in London for first foreign trip as Opposition leader
item.description: Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre is on a European tour that is taking him to the United Kingdom and Germany where he is meeting with like-minded politicians and laying out a political and economic vision he hopes can rival Prime Minister Mark Carney.

item.title: Canadian defence minister calls slain Iranian leader ‘force for evil,’ urges ceasefire
Did Carney go too far in offering 'support' for U.S. strikes against Iran?
item.description: Every prime minister is called upon, at one time or another, to comment on the actions of an American president. For Mark Carney, the latest moment of necessity arrived this past weekend — when the United States and Israel launched new attacks on Iran.

item.title: No more federal budget watchdog in Ottawa as interim PBO’s term expires
item.description: Interim PBO Jason Jacques was appointed to a six-month term in September that ended at 5 p.m. ET Monday without a successor in place.

item.title: Ontario teacher, education worker unions call for contract talks to start early
item.description: Contracts for Ontario’s teachers and education workers expire at the end of this summer and their union leaders are calling on the education minister to start the bargaining process early, saying issues such as class sizes need to be urgently addressed.

item.title: Ring of Fire roads to be complete by 2031, ahead of schedule: Ford
Doug Ford names new chief of staff with experience in Canada-U.S. relations
item.description: Travis Kann currently holds the dual role of principal secretary and head of Ontario-U.S. relations

item.title: Danielle Smith says Iran war underscores need for new Alberta pipeline to the coast
item.description: Danielle Smith says any disruption in the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil choke point at the mouth of the Persian Gulf, underscores the need for a new Alberta pipeline to the Pacific.

item.title: In London, Poilievre pitches new UK, Australia, New Zealand partnership
item.description: Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre is in Europe where he's arguing for tighter economic and security relations between Canada, New Zealand, Australia and its European allies.

item.title: Rain and sun to continue throughout week in Toronto
Submarine companies make their final pitches to Canada as it rushes to choose a fleet
Ontario and Nova Scotia to allow direct-to-consumer alcohol sales between provinces
item.description: Ontario and Nova Scotia will begin allowing consumers to buy alcohol directly from producers in each province as both jurisdictions look for ways to increase internal trade within Canada in the face of the U.S. trade war.

item.title: From shipping chaos to spiking oil prices, here’s how war in the Middle East is affecting Canadian business
item.description: The postFrom shipping chaos to spiking oil prices, here’s how war in the Middle East is affecting Canadian businessappeared first onThe Logic.

item.title: Feds release Musqueam title agreements covering much of greater Vancouver
item.description: OTTAWA - The federal government has released details of recent agreements with the Musqueam First Nation recognizing Aboriginal title over an …

item.title: Plans in works to replace Metro Toronto Convention Centre, Ford says
item.description: Toronto could soon be getting a modern new convention centre to replace its marquee convention space, Ontario Premier Doug Ford said Tuesday.

item.title: B.C. to adopt permanent daylight saving time, after springing forward one more time
item.description: B.C. Premier David Eby said that following the upcoming time change on Sunday morning, when the clocks will “spring forward,” there will be no further time changes.

item.title: Winnipegger charged with threatening prime minister on social media
item.description: A Winnipeg man faces charges after he allegedly posted threats against Prime Minister Mark Carney and incited hatred against Jews and Muslims.

item.title: Ontario teachers and Ford government are at odds — over when to begin contract talks
Airlines suspend flights to Middle East, as Anita Anand urges Canadians in Iran to shelter in place
Ottawa weighs splitting submarine contract between Europe and South Korea, sources say
item.description: Final proposals were received on Monday, and the government is set to make a procurement decision by April 4 at earliest

item.title: Union files grievance, asks feds to suspend early retirement program
Gardiner Expressway undergoes emergency repairs over fears of falling concrete — again
Doug Ford unveils design for new Science Centre at Ontario Place
What’s next for the sprawling ravine site of the shuttered Ontario Science Centre?
The number of Ontarians over 80 is about to surge. Advocates say the Ford government needs a strategy
What you need to know about new legislation that could expand First Nations status
Carney and India’s Modi strike new energy partnership
item.description: The two leaders announced a series of joint agreements, including a strategic energy partnership. Carney is currently in New Delhi.

item.title: Ottawa boosting support, but warns Canadians in Middle East to prepare contingency plans
item.description: Global Affairs says government’s ability to provide consular services during the conflict is limited

item.title: Toronto’s mayor promised to help these tenants. Months later, conditions are so bad Canada Post won’t deliver their mail
item.description: Tenants at 500 Dawes Rd. say they must travel 45 minutes to get their mail after Canada Post cancelled delivery to the building for the 'safet…

item.title: India dismisses allegation of link between Indian consulate and Nijjar assassination
item.description: Federal leaders currently in India on a trade mission did not respond to the revelations

item.title: Ontario Place, construction and the new Science Centre: Your questions answered
How Toronto is bracing for 300,000 World Cup fans — with just about 100 days before kickoff
Carney picks a realpolitik side on Iran war
item.description: The Prime Minister wasted no time backing U.S. military action to prevent the country from obtaining a nuclear weapon

item.title: How Doug Ford got pulled into a fight over a Toronto hockey arena and youth centre — and why this faceoff may be ending
Deadline day: $20B submarine showdown puts Korean and German bids in spotlight
item.description: Canada's high-speed push to replace its aging Victoria-class submarines reaches a pivotal moment on Monday as South Korea's Hanwha Ocean and Germany's TKMS submit formal bids in a program worth more than $20 billion. The contest — to supply up to 12 boats — will shape Canada's naval reach and industrial alliances for decades.

item.title: After a bumpy ride, federal budget watchdog steps down with some praise
item.description: During his short tenure as Canada's interim parliamentary budget officer, Jason Jacques endured accusations of partisanship, criticism over his message delivery and, in the end, praise for representing an institution now ranked first among its international equals.

item.title: Union urges federal government to halt early retirement program for public servants
item.description: The Public Service Alliance of Canada wants the federal government to suspend the early retirement program for public servants and has filed complaints to the Federal Public Sector Labour Relations and Employment Board. Announced in the last budget, the program is intended to help alleviate job cuts as thousands of public sector jobs are set to be eliminated by 2029.

item.title: Wary U.S. allies try to avoid getting drawn into criticizing Washington or attacking Tehran
item.description: Washington's Western allies have stressed they were not involved in the U.S.-Israeli attack on Iran — but they've also carefully skirted judgment of its legality.

item.title: Mark Carney walks a tightrope heading into meeting with India’s Narendra Modi
Carney signs deals worth billions in diplomatic breakthrough with India's Modi
item.description: In a statement to reporters after a one-on-one meeting with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi, Carney said Canada is going all-in on diversifying trade. The two countries have set a goal to more than double two-way trade to some $70 billion a year by 2030, he said, as Canada continues a push to reduce its dependence on the U.S.

item.title: Carney secures $2.6-billion uranium supply deal with India, launches talks on trade deal
item.description: After months of discussions, meeting between Carney and Modi marks a thaw in relations

item.title: PM Carney lands in New Delhi, plans to meet Modi tomorrow
Abel Mwansa Jr. shot last month in Tumbler Ridge, B.C. to be buried in Zambia Monday
B.C. Conservative MPs Zimmer, Caputo ask for public inquiry into Tumbler Ridge deaths
item.description: VICTORIA - Calls for an independent investigation into the shooting in Tumbler Ridge, B.C., where eight people, including six children were sh…

item.title: Conservatives call for federal independent inquiry on Tumbler Ridge mass shooting
item.description: The federal Conservatives are calling for an independent inquiry on the mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge, B.C., to understand what happened and how to prevent similar tragedies from occurring in the future.

item.title: Khamenei’s death met with ‘jubilation’ among Iranian-Canadians: Liberal MP
item.description: Iranian-Canadians are greeting news of Ayatollah Khamenei's death with jubilation, one MP says. Iran, meanwhile, is vowing revenge for U.S. and Israel's surprise bombing campaign.

item.title: Ottawa won’t say if it penalized Gripen jets in 2021 analysis
Poilievre says he has offered to go to U.S. to make Canada’s case
item.description: Conservative Leader has been under pressure to explain how he’d manage the U.S. trade dispute if he forms government

item.title: Did Canadian exchange officers participate in U.S. Iran strike planning? DND says no, but questions linger
item.description: A retired Canadian major-general says it's highly likely personnel on exchange with U.S. Central Command were likely involved at some level in planning and targeting airstrikes on Iran unless Ottawa explicitly barred participation. The Defence Department disputes the claim but has not answered the question of whether they were barred from participation.

item.title: Hailing India as a 'natural partner,' Carney says Canada can secure a free trade deal by year's end
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney's Indian charm offensive continued over the weekend as he set aside any notion of bilateral bad blood and hailed the South Asian country as a "natural partner."

item.title: Canada should not have an election before reviewing CUSMA trade deal, says Poilievre
item.description: Conservative Leader Pierre Polievre says Canada should not have an election before it reviews the Canada-U.S.-Mexico Agreement (CUSMA) this year — and instead he's reiterating his proposal the Carney government set up an all-party committee on the matter.

item.title: Canada poised to become 'one of the largest suppliers of LNG in the world': energy minister
item.description: Canada's energy minister says the country is poised to become one of the world's biggest suppliers of liquefied natural gas, exporting as much as 100 million tonnes per year.

item.title: Ottawa's Iranian diaspora shares hopes, fears in the wake of military strike
item.description: There's a mix of reactions from Iranian Canadians in Ottawa on Saturday in the wake of U.S. and Israeli airstrikes targeting their home country. Some feel the airstrikes are overdue, while some worry war won't bring peace.

item.title: Canada supports U.S. actions in destroying Iran's nuclear program, Carney says
item.description: As U.S. President Donald Trump charges ahead with a major attack on Iran, Prime Minister Mark Carney says Canada supports at least one component of the American mission: destroying Iran's nuclear program.

item.title: Canadian universities ink partnerships with Indian counterparts during Carney’s visit
item.description: Foreign Affairs Minister says pacts will expand research, student and faculty exchanges, AI centres

item.title: Israel launches fresh wave of attacks on Iran and Lebanon as Iranian drones hit U.S. Embassy in Saudi Arabia
He was hired to fight for Wales's future generations. Does Canada need a job like that?
item.description: For the last three years, Derek Walker has been advocating for the rights of Wales's youngest generations — and generations still to come. As Canadian youth grapple with issues like climate change, affordability and the rise of AI, advocates say it's time the federal government appoints its own champion for generational fairness.

item.title: Canadian passports are about to get more expensive
item.description: The cost of a Canadian passport is about to rise and plans by Prime Minister Mark Carney's government to overhaul the way it sets prices could result in them being even more expensive in the future.

item.title: No exemptions for India on foreign interference concerns, Anand says
item.description: Foreign Affairs Minister declines to clarify whether she believes India is still conducting foreign interference in Canada

item.title: Carney backs U.S. air strikes on Iran but won’t contribute militarily
item.description: Canadians in Iran should shelter in place, PM says

item.title: Ontario’s plan to increase the powers of special constables drawing mixed reviews
item.description: Ontario’s plan to give special constables additional powers, allowing them to arrest and charge people caught using drugs in public places, is drawing mixed reviews.

item.title: Politics Insider: Moe optimistic on uranium deal with India
item.description: India’s envoy to Canada said this week he expects announcement during Carney visit

item.title: Solomon to meet OpenAI CEO Altman in wake of mass killings in Tumbler Ridge, B.C.
item.description: The shooter used a ChatGPT account that was shut down last June after being flagged internally but it was not reported to authorities.

item.title: Senate seeks time limit on political parties’ privacy law
item.description: Bill C-4 amendment addresses provisions that exempt parties from provincial laws

item.title: Supplying India with uranium ‘very possible in near future,’ Premier Moe says
item.description: Saskatchewan and New Brunswick premiers join Carney on his first visit to India as Prime Minister

item.title: Six storeys, 10 units: On paper, it seemed the kind of housing Toronto wants. Then its Scarborough neighbours weighed in
Doug Ford blasts the ‘radical left’ at Toronto city hall over estimate on cost of replacing speed cameras
Poilievre makes the pivot on Trump, but repudiates Carney’s foreign policy
item.description: Conservative Leader called out the U.S. President, but didn’t go far enough to match the Canadian mood

item.title: Liberal budget bill passes House of Commons after months of debate
item.description: The more-than-600-page bill now moves to the Senate

item.title: Alberta budget 2026 comes with spending hikes but $9.4B deficit
item.description: Premier Danielle Smith’s UCP government has introduced a new Alberta budget that promises more money for health and education but also an eye-popping deficit of $9.4 billion.

item.title: Liberals survive confidence vote as House passes budget implementation bill
item.description: The legislation enacts key measures proposed in the Liberals' 2025 federal budget, which passed a confidence vote for Prime Minister Mark Carney's government in November last year.

item.title: Poilievre calls Trump’s Canada attacks ‘wrong,’ urges U.S. trade stability
item.description: Poilievre called for the creation of an all-party working group on the Canada-U.S.-Mexico Agreement on trade as the three countries begin a review of the deal.

item.title: Liberal, Bloc Québécois rematch in Terrebonne riding after Supreme Court nixes result
item.description: Bloc Leader Yves-François Blanchet announced his candidate Thursday, paying tribute to Sinclair-Desgagné's relentless drive.

item.title: Poilievre pitches critical-mineral stockpile as leverage in trade talks with the U.S.
item.description: The postPoilievre pitches critical-mineral stockpile as leverage in trade talks with the U.S.appeared first onThe Logic.

item.title: PM urged to clarify whether he believes India still carrying out interference in Canada
item.description: A senior government official previously told reporters Ottawa does not believe India is still meddling in Canadian politics and orchestrating violence

item.title: Ontario reveals design for new science centre at Toronto’s waterfront, says it could be ready in 3 years
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford pulled back the curtain Thursday on the province’s design for a new science centre at Toronto’s waterfront, calling it a “world-class” facility that will be “jaw-dropping.”

item.title: Doug Ford takes issue with Toronto's 13-year timeline to build road safety measures
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford took issue Thursday with a City of Toronto report that concluded it would take upwards of 13 years and cost $52 million to build speed bumps and other traffic calming measures in school zones.

item.title: Federal government commits $1.55-billion for First Nations children
item.description: Ottawa will extend Jordan’s Principle, which provides health and education support, for one year

item.title: Doug Ford hints he’s ready to amalgamate Niagara’s 12 cities and towns
Canadians on Mexican trade mission undeterred by drug violence
item.description: The postCanadians on Mexican trade mission undeterred by drug violenceappeared first onThe Logic.
